rulings.Case Title: SPRINT TELEPHONY PCS, L.P. et al VS. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Mtn For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S MOTION For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative MOTION For Summary Adjudication. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Revenue and Taxation Code § 5148 contemplates a claim for refund procedure. (Rev. & Tax. Code § 5148, subd. (f) & (g).) Plaintiff's interpretation of the statute would render language superfluous. "We do not presume that the Legislature performs idle acts, nor do we construe statutory provisions so as to render them superfluous." (Shoemaker v. Myers (1990) 52 Cal.3d 1, 22.) Subdivisions (f) and (g) are harmonized, and not disjunctive. (See Essayli v. Department of Motor Vehicles (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 1251, 1255-1256.) Plaintiffs did not affirmatively state that their petition was to serve as claim for refund. (UMF no. 5.) By not checking either the "Yes" box , Plaintiff did not affirmatively state that the petition constituted a claim for refund. (Rev. & Tax. Code § 5148(f).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL LONG VS. SAN FRANCISCO 49ERS LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORTY NINERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court denies summary judgment and summary adjudication. Defendant's notice of motion is defective because it does not describe the causes of action that are to be adjudicated. Defendant's separate statement does not comply with CRC 3.1350(d) or (h) because it does not describe the causes of action to be adjudicated. In addition, defendant failed to maintain its initial summary judgment burden because it did not address all of the theories of liability alleged in the amended complaint. For example, defendant ignores plaintiff's allegation that defendant negligently failed to warn patrons of the prior shooting. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA ROSA VS. MORGAN STANLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order To Reclassify Case As Limited Civil Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT GLENBOROUGH NEW MONTGOMERY, LLC'S Motion For An Order To Reclassify Case As Limited Civil Action. Motion denied. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that Plaintiff's recovery over $25,000 is "virtually unattainable." (See Walker v. Sup. Ct. (1991) 63 Cal.3d 257, 269. Plaintiff has approximately $12,000 in medical bills, is still currently seeking medical treatment for an update on her medical condition, and still has attendant pain, stress, and related emotional distress damages related to the incident. (See Declaration of Rosa in Support of Opposition to Defendant's Motion to Reclassify Case 3-7.) rulings.Case Title: ANDREA LEUNG, TRUSTEE OF THE JEFFREY CHIN TRUST et al VS. NELLY D CHIN, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT ANDREA LEUNG, TRUSTEE OF THE JEFFREY CHIN TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is overruled. The statute of frauds (CC 1624(a)(1)) bars an oral contract that "by its terms is not to be performed within a year of the making thereof." The allegations of the cross complaint do not conclusively show that the contract runs afoul of the statute of frauds. There is a factual issue regarding the precise terms of the agreement. Even if the contract was for personal services and terminated upon death, paragraph 13 alleges that the parties "reiterated" the contract and paragraph 27 alleges that they "verbally affirmed" the contract. Accordingly, the parties ratified the prior contract and/or entered into a new agreement. The statute of limitations argument fails because paragraph 17 alleges that the breach occurred within the last two years prior to the filing of the action. The demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled. Conversion and fraud are distinct causes of action and conversion does not need to be pleaded with the same particularity as an intentional misrepresentation claim. There is no statute of limitations issue because paragraph 33 alleges that the conversion was discovered within the past 3 years prior to the filing of the action. The Court overrules the demurrer to the third cause of action. Nothing in the cross-complaint shows that the claim is time barred. It is unclear when Ms. Chin made the expenditures and paragraph 17 indicates that there is no statute of limitations issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DOLORES CANEPA VS. FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANTS TONY CLIFFORD & EDWIN ARRIOLA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the seventh cause of action is overruled. Supervisory employees may be held liable for torts that result in physical injury - i.e. intentional infliction of emotional distress. (See Chin, et al., Cal. Practice Guide: Employment Litigation (The Rutter Group) Ch. 5-M [5:896] (citing Sheppard v. Freeman (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 339, 343 fn. 4.).) Furthermore, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendants' conduct was extreme and outrageous. (See FAC 44-48.). Also, Defendants have failed to demonstrate, as a matter of law, that their conduct did not contravene fundamental public policy or exceed the risks inherent in an employment relationship. (See Livitsanos v. Superior Court, 2 Cal. 4th 744, 754 (1992).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DOLORES CANEPA VS. FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is overruled as to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, and seventh causes of action, and is sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Defendant's demurrer to the second, third, fourth and fifth causes of action are overruled, as Defendant has not demonstrated, as a matter of law, that Plaintiff's Fair Employment and Housing Act claims ("FEHA") are entirely preempted by 12 U.S.C. § 24, Fifth (the "National Bank Act"). Defendant's demurrer to the sixth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend as state law claims by a bank officer regarding breach of an employment agreement are entirely preempted by Section 24, Fifth of the National Bank Act. (See Wells Fargo Bank v. Superior Court, 53 Cal. 3d 1082, 1087-88 (1991).) Defendant's demurrer to the seventh cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendant's conduct, by and through its agents, was extreme and outrageous. (See FAC 44-48.). Also, Defendant has failed to demonstrate, as a matter of law, that its conduct did not contravene fundamental public policy or exceed the risks in an inherent employment relationship. (See Livitsanos v. Superior Court, 2 Cal. 4th 744, 754 (1992).) Defendant's demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled as it is not a FEHA or employment contract claim, and Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action for retaliation under Labor Code § 1102.5. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT BARWORKS, INC.'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. This motion is off calendar. Insufficient service per CCP § 1005, which requires at least 16 court days, and an additional 5 calendar days for notice of hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CONNIE H. SEBASTIAN VS. SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend. The complaint is uncertain, and does not state material facts showing Defendant violated any laws. The complaint does refer to California Insurance Code §790.03 and Bus. & Prof. Code § 17200. However, no private right of action exists for violation of the Unfair Insurance Practices Act (UIPA), and enforcement powers are vested exclusively in the Insurance Commissioner. Moradi Shalal v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Cos. (1988), 46 Cal.3d 287, 304. Thus, plaintiff is given leave to amend to state allegations necessary to plead a claim for violation of the UCL. Plaintiff must also state facts necessary to plead elder abuse. Plaintiff must clearly plead the essential facts for each cause of action, as well as state the causes of action on which Plaintiff seeks relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID KLONOFF MD VS. CELINE TAMIR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT CELINE TAMIR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is overruled as to both causes of action. Defendant's demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendant delivered a written letter to Plaintiff's counsel threatening to expose Plaintiff's alleged crime to the IRS, intending to use fear of exposure to get Defendant's divorce case to settle and to get money or property from Plaintiff. (See Plaintiff's Complaint 13-18.) Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to show that Defendant's conduct was outrageous, Defendant intended to cause Plaintiff emotional distress, and Defendant suffered emotional distress as the result of Defendant's conduct. (See Plaintiff's Complaint 20-24.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DAVID KLONOFF MD VS. CELINE TAMIR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT CELINE TAMIR'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant's Special Motion to Strike is denied as to both causes of action. The evidence establishes that Defendant's alleged conduct does not arise from protected activity, as it is illegal extortion. (See Flatley v. Mauro, 39 Cal. 4th 299, 320, (2006).) Threats or accusations of violations of the law are sufficient to show the element of fear by induced threat under Cal. Penal Code. § 519. (See Flatley, 39 Cal. 4th at 332.) Moreover, any party, who with the intent to extort, sends any letter implying threats of a crime under Cal. Penal Code § 519, is punishable in the same manner as if such money or property were actually obtained by means of such threat. (See Cal. Penal Code § 523.) The evidence shows that Defendant sent a written demand letter to Plaintiff's counsel on April 25, 2013, with the intent to extort Plaintiff by inducing fear by threat of exposing Plaintiff's crime to the Internal Revenue Service. (Declaration of McReynolds in Support of Special Motion to Strike and Recover Costs and Fees Under CCP Section 425,16, Exh. A, pgs. 7-8.) If the case did not settle, Defendant would also seek contribution fees from Plaintiff. (See Id.) Because Defendant has not made a prima facie showing that the alleged conduct arises out of protected activity, Plaintiff does not need to establish a probability of prevailing on the merits. Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67. Plaintiff's request for attorney fees and costs is denied, as the evidence does not show that Defendant's special motion to was frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. (See CCP § 425.16.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
